ideas.title: Essay: 'Circular capital and local compute' — Jevons, DePIN, and the factory's own Spark offload
ideas.why: Grounds the 'Jevons Paradox' and 'DePIN' concepts against the live world signal ('GPU-boom financing scrutiny' + 'decentralized/distributed AI compute'), teaching one transferable lesson from the factory's real Spark-vs-cloud economics — content, not another widget.

ideas.title: ⌘K command palette for search, post jump, and concept lookup
ideas.why: Expresses the 'Agent-computer interface (ACI)' and 'Context ontology' concepts as effortless content discovery (borrowing the ⌘K pattern from Linear and Vercel docs) — a real navigation gap for a growing post/concept corpus, and explicitly allowed (palette, not a shell).

ideas.title: Live Multi-Agent Debate / trajectory visualizer of the idea-engine
ideas.why: Builds on the 'Multi-Agent Debate', 'Agent trajectory' and 'Reflexion' concepts by animating the propose→gate→ship→verify loop as a legible graph, answering the world signal's appetite for 'minimal, legible agent implementations that demystify what an agent actually is'.

ideas.title: 3D replayable pipeline-run timeline mapped onto the WebGL brain graph
ideas.why: Directly grounds the ontology's 'Agent Session Replay', 'Time-travel debugging' and 'Agent execution tracing' concepts in a scrubbable 3D map of each dark-factory run, mirroring the world signal 'Show HN: Mindwalk – Replay coding-agent sessions on a 3D map' — visitors watch the agents propose→gate→ship node by node.

ideas.title: Respect prefers-reduced-motion on the WebGL brain graph and animated tiles
ideas.why: A genuine accessibility fix grounded in the 'CSS color-scheme'/front-end tech-stack concepts — pause or degrade the force-directed graph animation when the OS requests reduced motion, so the destination is usable for motion-sensitive readers.

ideas.title: Related-posts by shared-tag scoring as a 100%-covered pure fn in blog-logic
ideas.why: Deepens 'Software cartography' and 'Social distribution' by adding real content discovery (borrowing Gwern's related-links pattern) as a tested pure function behind the coverage gate — safe because the logic lives in blog-logic, not a fragile main.rs multiline edit.
